response.justification: While Wei demonstrates strong full-stack engineering skills and meaningful infrastructure experience handling large-scale distributed systems, the resume has a critical gap: only ~2–3 years of C++ experience versus the stated 8-year minimum requirement. Recent career trajectory shows a clear pivot to TypeScript/Node.js and away from systems-level programming. Leadership and mentoring credentials are present but lack the cloud-specific or DevOps engineering focus the role prefers.
strengths: Proven experience operating large-scale distributed systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/sec) and shipping multiple production products
Demonstrated technical leadership through staff roles, open-source project ownership, and workshop design reflecting strong grasp of testing, architecture, and software craftsmanship
Versatile engineer comfortable across multiple tech stacks with evidence of establishing best practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and mentoring peers
concerns: Critical shortfall on minimum C++ requirement (~2–3 years actual vs. 8 required), with no recent systems-level programming work
Career momentum is away from systems and compute platforms, recently pivoting to full-stack web and AI-agent infrastructure rather than cloud infrastructure leadership
No demonstrated expertise in cloud-compute platforms, production cloud architecture balancing trade-offs, or formal mentoring of cloud/DevOps engineers
